candidate.title: Huawei Atlas 800T A3 aggregate training-server fields

identity_keys.title: Huawei Atlas 800T A3 official product page

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Read the existing local Huawei Atlas 800T A3 product-page capture. The technical table states one Atlas 800T A3 server has 8 Ascend 910 processors and 8 x 128 GB on-chip memory, plus additional aggregate system values. No network fetch was used.
local_checks.wiki_search: Searched compute accelerator catalog, parameter comparison, coverage map, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, ingest.md, and prior manifests for Atlas 800T A3, huawei-atlas-800t, aggregate_memory, cloud_accelerator_count, 8 Ascend, and 8 x 128 GB. Existing pages used Atlas 800T A3 only as raw aggregate comparison or boundary evidence; no structured SKU row or resolved aggregate fields existed.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md routes this hardware catalog slice to Compute Accelerator Spec Catalog, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, and AI Infra Coverage Map. No standalone page was needed.
local_checks.prior_manifest_scan: Parent-14 explicitly avoided Atlas 800T A3 while promoting Atlas 300I A2 card variants. Parent-17 promoted only AWS Trn2 aggregate memory. Earlier hardware manifests mention Atlas 800T A3 as an aggregate/system boundary, not as a resolved catalog row.

duplicate_boundary_checks.result: Parent-14 says no Huawei Atlas 800T A3 or aggregate system claim was promoted while resolving Atlas 300I A2 card variants.
Parent-17 resolves aggregate_memory only for the existing AWS Trn2 cloud-offering row and does not mention Atlas 800T A3.
Before parent-21, Atlas 800T A3 appeared only in the aggregate raw comparison row; the structured catalog did not resolve cloud_accelerator_count or aggregate_memory.
Before parent-21, hardware notes kept aggregate systems unresolved and had no known_source entry for Atlas 800T A3 aggregate catalog fields.

gap_reason: The local official Atlas 800T A3 product page contains source-visible aggregate system fields that fit existing schema fields without changing the schema: cloud_accelerator_count=8 and aggregate_memory=1024 GB. Promoting only those fields improves hardware coverage while preserving aggregate compute, fabric, storage, cooling, benchmark, and operation claims as boundary evidence.

promoted_fields.boundary: Aggregate training-server count from one Atlas 800T A3 server; not a single-card metric.
Derived only from source-stated 8 x 128 GB on-chip memory; memory bandwidth remains comparison-only.

non_promoted_boundaries: 6.0 PFLOPS FP16 and 12.0 POPS INT8 remain aggregate system compute values, not single-accelerator resolved fields.

3.2 TB/s memory bandwidth, 784 GB/s D2D bandwidth, 8 x 400GE RoCE, 56 x 400GE bus-protocol interfaces, PCIe 5.0 expansion slots, local storage, power, cooling, fans, dimensions, and operating-temperature values remain boundary evidence.

No Atlas 300I A2, AWS Trn2, Kunlunxin R480-X8, DPU, inference runtime, orchestration, data/RAG, security, network/storage, crawler code, backend/frontend, dashboard, source profile, or subscription was modified as a new factual promotion.
